content.problemStatement:
Chef is given two binary strings AAA and BBB, each having length NNN.

Chef wants to find the number of binary strings CCC, of length NNN, such that H(A,C)=H(B,C)H(A, C) = H(B, C)H(A,C)=H(B,C), where H(X,Y)H(X, Y)H(X,Y) denotes the hamming distance between the strings XXX and YYY.

Since the answer can be large, output it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.

Note: Hamming distance between two strings of equal length is the number of positions at which the corresponding characters are different.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the length of the strings.
The second line contains the binary string AAA.
The third line contains the binary string BBB.




Output Format
For each test case, output in a new line, the number of binary strings CCC which satisfy the given conditions modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: AAA and BBB consist of 000 and 111 only.
Sum of NNN over all test cases do not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The number of strings CCC, of length 222, such that H(A,C)=H(B,C)H(A, C) = H(B, C)H(A,C)=H(B,C) is 222. The strings are:

C=10C = 10C=10: Here, H(11,10)=H(00,10)=1H(11, 10) = H(00, 10) = 1H(11,10)=H(00,10)=1, as there is only one position where the corresponding characters are different.
C=01C = 01C=01: Here, H(11,01)=H(00,01)=1H(11, 01) = H(00, 01) = 1H(11,01)=H(00,01)=1.

Test case 333: The number of strings CCC, of length 333, such that H(A,C)=H(B,C)H(A, C) = H(B, C)H(A,C)=H(B,C) is 444. The strings are:

C=000C = 000C=000: Here, H(101,000)=H(011,000)=2H(101, 000) = H(011, 000) = 2H(101,000)=H(011,000)=2, as there are two positions where the corresponding characters are different.
C=111C = 111C=111: Here, H(101,111)=H(011,111)=1H(101, 111) = H(011, 111) = 1H(101,111)=H(011,111)=1, as there is only one position where the corresponding characters are different.
C=001C = 001C=001: Here, H(101,001)=H(011,001)=1H(101, 001) = H(011, 001) = 1H(101,001)=H(011,001)=1, as there is only one position where the corresponding characters are different.
C=110C = 110C=110: Here, H(101,110)=H(011,110)=2H(101, 110) = H(011, 110) = 2H(101,110)=H(011,110)=2, as there are two positions where the corresponding characters are different.
